REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR CROSS-COMPLAINANTS TO PROPERLY STATE THEIR CAUSES OF ACTION. EACH CAUSE OF ACTION SHALL BE SEPARATELY LABELED AND EACH ELEMENT OF EACH CAUSE OF ACTION MUST BE SUPPORTED BY FACTS. CONCLUSORY ALLEGATIONS ARE NOT SUFFICIENT. CROSS-COMPLAINANTS SHALL HAVE 10 DAYS FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER TO FILE AN AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B) JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Off Calendar Feb-23-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF BARRETT DAFFIN FRAPPIER TREDER & WEISS, LLP Motion For: (1) Statutory Discharge; (2) Payment Of Attorneys Fees And Costs; And (3) Deposit Of Funds In Court;

is GRANTED. Plaintiff shall recover $7,334.43 in fees and cost; deposit the remainder of the surplus funds with the Court; and be discharged from this case upon said deposit.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-29-2026 Continued To Apr-14-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Petition And Notice Of Motion; Motion For Distribution Of Surplus Proceeds
Off Calendar Feb-03-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Pamela J. Palmieri for defendant and cross-complainant Sharon Drummond.
Alexander Lauvray for plaintiff Barrett Daffin Frappier Treder & Weiss, LLP (via Zoom).
Nicholas Matayron for defendant and cross-defendant Citibank, N.A. (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on defendant's motion for distribution of surplus proceeds.

The Court addressed issues raised in its tentative ruling regarding the use of quoted language and supporting authorities in defendant's reply papers and clarified that timeliness was not at issue. Ms. Palmieri acknowledged citation errors and addressed the sources relied upon for the arguments concerning filing and receipt dates.

Mr. Lauvray addressed plaintiff's claim for attorneys' fees and costs and argued that any distribution of surplus proceeds must account for those claims. Mr. Matayron addressed its position as a lienholder.

Following argument, the Court ruled as follows:

DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR DISTRIBUTION OF SURPLUS PROCEEDS is denied.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

Petition And Notice Of Motion; Motion For Distribution Of Surplus Proceeds
Off Calendar Dec-29-2025 Continued To Feb-03-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Pamela Palmieri counsel for defendant Sharon Drummond appearing via Zoom.
Alex Lauvray counsel for plaintiff appearing via Zoom.
Charles Mumley counsel for Citibank appearing via Court Call.

The tentative to continue is not contested but the date to continue is as Ms. Palmieri will not be available. All parties agree to continue matter to Feb-03-2026. Ms. Palmieri to notice the parties. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR DISTRIBUTION OF SURPLUS PROCEEDS is continued to February 3, 2026. No later than January 9, 2026 counsel for the moving party to file a declaration attaching a copy of CRC 2.259 with the following portions quoted in the reply to Citibank's opposition highlighted: "a document is deemed filed on the date it is received by the court" and "the filing date of the corrected document relates back to the date the document was originally received by the court." (Reply 2:13-16). Also attached to the declaration shall be a copy of the reply in 12-point font.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: L. Young; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-22-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Pamela Palmieri; 916-899-3669; counsel for defendant/cross complainant Sharon Drummond appearing via Zoom.
Timothy Frenzel; defendant in pro per.
Nicolas Matayron; 949-252-9400; Malcolm Cisneros; counsel for Citibank appearing via Zoom.

Ms. Palmieri seeks clarification on how to proceed. Discussed. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT CITIBANK, N.A., SUCCESSOR BY MERGER TO CITIBANK (WEST), FSB DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Cross-Complainants to properly state their causes of action. Each cause of action shall be separately labeled and each element of each cause of action must be supported by facts. Conclusory allegations are not sufficient. Cross-Complainants shall have 10 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended Cross-Complaint.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
Judge: Charles F. Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)
